docs: https://docs-api.tinyemail.com/ (readme.io-hosted reference; also linked from https://tinyemail.com/api and https://docs.tinyemail.com/)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. Ground truth is tinyEmail's own readme.io-hosted API reference (docs-api.tinyemail.com), fetched directly (its embedded page-sidebar JSON was parsed to enumerate every reference/endpoint page tinyEmail itself has published). That reference declares EXACTLY ONE documented endpoint in the entire product: `POST /segment/customer` ('Import Customer', under the 'Customers' category) — now implemented as the `create_subscriber` write action below. Independent corroboration: tinyEmail's API is explicitly described (per tinyEmail support/community sources) as an Enterprise-only, request-access feature organized around 'Campaigns and Contacts' with 'Contact Members' (list subscriptions) and 'Sender Details', consistent with a deliberately minimal, mostly write-only integration surface (a marketing-automation import/upsert endpoint, not a full data-export API) rather than an under-documented one this review failed to find more of. `GET /v1/subscribers`, `/v1/lists`, and `/v1/campaigns` — the 3 read streams this bundle has carried since its original migration — do NOT appear anywhere in tinyEmail's own published API reference; they are preserved completely unchanged for parity (conventions.md §5's meta-rule: never change accepted-input behavior for an existing stream) on the working assumption that they reflect a real, working, but undocumented/legacy tinyEmail API surface the original connector author had access to (plausibly an earlier or account-specific API generation predating the current public reference). This bundle does not fabricate additional undocumented endpoints beyond what already existed pre-Pass-B; the ONE new capability added here (`create_subscriber`) is the ONE endpoint tinyEmail itself actually publishes.

excluded.reason: no automations resource of any kind is documented anywhere in tinyEmail's own published API reference (docs-api.tinyemail.com); this path was carried in a prior version of this file as a hypothetical Pass B target but could not be corroborated against any real source and is removed as a fabricated guess
